license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rvvd666
title: Giant Unilamellar Vesicles (GUVs) Preparation by Electroformation Method
author: Zehra Kahveci

steps_raw.instruction: Weigh phospholipid of interest, dissolve the powder in chloroform at 10 mM final concentration.
For fluorescent GUVs, add fluorescent phospholipid Texas-Red-DHPE (1 mM stock) to reach the final molar ratio of 1:500.
Identify the conductive sides of the ITO-slides with a multimeter.
Use a 5 μl Hamilton syringe to spread 10 μl of phospholipid stock solution on one of the ITO glass slide.
Leave the ITO coating side upwards into a vacuum dessicator for 15 min.
Prepare 195 mM sucrose solution, dissolve it in Hepes Buffer 5 mM pH 7.4, vortex until sucrose is dissolved.
Spread a thin layer of vacuum grease evenly on the O-ring that you use.
After the ITO-slides are dried, take the top part off from the Nanion Vesicle Prep Pro chamber by removing the screws.
Use tweezers to place the ITO-slide lipid film point upwards in the Nanion Vesicle Prep Pro.
Place an O-ring around the dried lipid film, add 280 μl fof 195 mM sucrose solution into the O-ring.

Place a second ITO-slide on top of the O-ring with the conductive side facing downwards using tweezers. Note that, the right end of the top slide has to touch properly to the electrode on the right-side.
Place the top part of the chamber back on and screw it carefully.

Turn-on the Nanion VPP, select the appropriate protocol and initiate the formation process. 00:05:00, Frequency 5 Hz, applied voltage from 0 to 3 V 02:00:00, Frequency 5 Hz, applied voltage 3 V 00:05:00, Frequency 5 Hz, applied voltage from 3V to 0 Undetermined
After the protocol ends remove the screws and take off the top part of the chamber. Use the tweezers to remove the top ITO-slide.
Cut a pipette tip and collect the sample from the ITO-slide surface slowly.
Withdraw a 5 µl aliquot of the electroformation product and inspect the GUVs under an epifluorescence microscope.
